input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property, which `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets to `true` automatically:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**Why this works:** Any project that references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` gets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Your library projects never reference that package, so the condition is `false` for them.

**Caveat:** `IsTestProject` is set by the SDK *after* `Directory.Build.props` is imported, so this block should go in **`Directory.Build.targets`** instead if the condition isn't resolving. The `.targets` file is imported later and guarantees `IsTestProject` is already set:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets (preferred, more reliable) -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

An alternative, if you prefer `Directory.Build.props`, is a naming-convention condition:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  ...
</PropertyGroup>
```

This works earlier in evaluation but is fragile if your naming isn't consistent. The `Directory.Build.targets` + `IsTestProject` approach is the most robust.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for something unique to test projects — typically the presence of the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package reference or a naming convention.

The most robust approach is to use `IsTestProject`, which is set automatically by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. Let me give a concise answer.
